steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Harper's speaker criticizes activists who champion causes in other countries while overlooking local concerns. The correct quotation must show both halves of that contrast: attention to distant/foreign suffering and neglect of nearby/home suffering.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Men may tread down the poor and lowly...': describes divine justice grinding out the fate of oppressors. No foreign-versus-domestic contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — 'When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores, / Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors.': 'far-distant shores' names the foreign focus; 'your own doors' names the local suffering being ignored. The demand to 'remember' home-front tragedy directly embodies Harper's criticism. ✓
  • Choice C — 'God bless our native land...': a patriotic blessing with no contrast between foreign and domestic causes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Let me make the songs for the people...': describes the power of song, with no reference to foreign versus local concerns. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
